source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A metal rod ab of length $10\mathrm{cm}$ is horizontally suspended in a uniform magnetic field by two identical springs; the magnetic induction strength of the field is $0.1\mathrm{T}$, directed perpendicularly into the plane of the paper; the upper ends of the springs are fixed, and the lower ends are insulated from the metal rod. The metal rod is connected through a switch to a battery with an electromotive force of $12\mathrm{V}$, and the total resistance of the circuit is $2\Omega$. It is known that when the switch is open, the elongation of each spring is $0.5\mathrm{cm}$; after closing the switch and the system reaching equilibrium again, the elongation of each spring changes by $0.3\ \mathrm{cm}$ compared to when the switch is open. The gravitational acceleration is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. Determine the direction of the Ampere force acting on the metal rod after the switch is closed.
2. What is the mass of the metal rod?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
The direction is vertically downward.
0.01 kg

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is intended to rely on the diagram
Question asks for the direction of the Ampere force; this typically depends on the current direction/polarity shown in the figure, which is not fully specified in text alone
An image is registered and available (image_count=1, registry_passed=true)
multi_step: The task has two linked subquestions: determine force direction, then compute rod mass
Solving mass requires combining circuit calculation (I=E/R), magnetic force magnitude (BIL), and spring-extension equilibrium changes
The spring elongation before and after closing the switch must be compared, so the solution requires at least multiple condition-combination steps rather than direct lookup
verifiability: Expected outputs are a discrete direction and a numeric mass, both of which are checkable against physics formulas
Registry passed with no listed issues, so the sample assets are present for downstream verification

risk_flags: force-direction judgment appears diagram-dependent because battery/current orientation is not fully recoverable from text alone
short_text answer contains two parts, so formatting/paraphrase variance may occur during exact-match style evaluation

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and required diagram are all present, so the core semantics are well supported.
Registry passed with no listed issues, indicating the sample assets are intact.
Initial scores are strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability, which makes this a valuable candidate.
Although force-direction judgment is diagram-dependent, the diagram is available, so this is not a blocking asset gap.
There is some evaluation-format risk because the short-text answer has two parts, but this affects scoring convenience more than sample recoverability.
